aids.title: FIFO Order
aids.content: **F**irst **I**n, **F**irst **O**ut. Think of a queue at a ticket counter -- the person who arrives first gets served first.
aids.forRemembering: The fundamental ordering principle of a queue

aids.title: Queue Operations: EDPI
aids.content: **E**nqueue (add at rear), **D**equeue (remove from front), **P**eek (view front element), **I**sEmpty (check if queue is empty). Remember: Every Day People Inquire at a queue.

aids.title: Linear Queue Wastage
aids.content: Imagine a bus where once a passenger exits from the front, their seat is blocked off and nobody can sit there again. The bus fills up from the back even though front seats are empty. That is linear queue memory wastage.
aids.forRemembering: Why linear queues waste memory and why circular queues are needed

aids.title: Queue Applications: BPS
aids.content: **B**FS (graph traversal), **P**rint spooling, **S**cheduling (CPU ready queue, disk scheduling). Anything that requires fair first-come-first-served ordering uses a queue.

aids.title: Queue vs Stack: FIFO vs LIFO
aids.content: **Queue = FIFO** (First In, First Out) like a ticket line. **Stack = LIFO** (Last In, First Out) like a stack of plates. Remember: Queue lines FORward, Stacks LIFe Off.
aids.forRemembering: The key difference between queue and stack ordering principles

aids.title: Queue Types: LCDP
aids.content: **L**inear (basic FIFO), **C**ircular (wraps around), **D**eque (double-ended), **P**riority (priority-based). Remember: Lazy Cats Drink Pillow (Linear, Circular, Deque, Priority).
aids.forRemembering: The four main types of queues covered in the syllabus

aids.title: Overflow vs Underflow
aids.content: **Overflow**: Queue is FULL (rear == MAX-1), cannot add more -- like an overflowing glass. **Underflow**: Queue is EMPTY (front > rear), cannot remove -- like trying to drink from an empty glass.
aids.forRemembering: The difference between overflow and underflow conditions

aids.title: Circular Queue Wrap
aids.content: In a circular queue, when rear reaches the end, it WRAPS AROUND to the beginning if there are empty slots at front. Think of a round dining table -- you keep going around and around.
aids.forRemembering: How circular queue solves the memory wastage problem

aids.title: Deque: Double-Ended
aids.content: **Deque** = Double-Ended Queue. You can insert and delete at BOTH ends (front and rear). Input-restricted: insertion at both, deletion at one. Output-restricted: deletion at both, insertion at one.
aids.forRemembering: How a deque allows operations at both ends

aids.title: Priority Queue Types
aids.content: **Ascending Priority Queue**: Smallest element has HIGHEST priority (comes out first). **Descending Priority Queue**: Largest element has HIGHEST priority (comes out first).
aids.forRemembering: The difference between ascending and descending priority queues
